description: Verified candidate tasks for benchmark v2 (see Docs/aether_benchmark_gaps.md §7). Splits release_board's kitchen-sink sub-skills into single-skill tasks (real-mean, clamp/normalize, classify-by-threshold, count-by-category), fills untested holes (clamp builtin, recursion, isolated real-mean), and introduces a negative-invariant tier (should_fail) that pins the compiler's deliberate rejections. Every positive reference_solution was compiled and run against build/bin/aether and matched expected_stdout exactly; every negative program was confirmed rejected with the listed error code. Negative tasks require a should_fail scorer path: compile the program, pass iff it is rejected with expected_error_code (no model generation).

tasks.title: Real-valued mean with fixed precision
tasks.prompt: Given three integer scores 70, 80, and 95, print exactly two lines. First line: `total = <sum>` where <sum> is their sum. Second line: `average = <mean>` where <mean> is their arithmetic mean rounded to exactly two decimal places.

tasks.title: Clamp values into a range
tasks.prompt: Print, one per line and in this order, each of these values clamped to the inclusive range 0 to 100: first 120, then -5, then 50.

tasks.title: Classify scores by threshold
tasks.prompt: For each of the scores 95, 83, 71, and 40 (in that order), print its letter grade on its own line: `A` for 90 or above, `B` for 80 to 89, `C` for 70 to 79, otherwise `F`.

tasks.title: Count pass/fail by threshold
tasks.prompt: Four students scored 75, 50, 90, and 40. A score of 60 or above passes; below 60 fails. Print `passed = <count>` on the first line and `failed = <count>` on the second.

tasks.title: Recursive factorial
tasks.prompt: Using a recursive function, compute the factorial of 5 and print it exactly in the form `5! = <value>`.

tasks.title: Compiler rejects inclusive range ..=
tasks.note: Aether ranges are half-open; ..= must be rejected. Verified: compiler emits [SYN-001] Unexpected token EQUAL.

tasks.title: Compiler rejects effectful call outside fx
tasks.note: The effect system's core invariant: an effectful call (println) must be inside an fx block. Verified: compiler emits [FX-001] Aether effect error: call to 'println' requires an fx block.
